text:
Hey cs188 I hope you read and pin this bullshit English teacher style theory I had on the video:

Watching the video, I got the idea that the male narrator was trapped in purgatory.
The blue sphere at the beginning of the video is supposed to represent Earth and the light from the "riding on the internet" clip represents the light of Heaven, which altogether give me vibes of the male narrator leaving Earth and ascending to Heaven. Then the light disappears and the video properly begins. I see this as the light of Heaven being ripped away from the male narrator as he is placed into purgatory.
When he says "wait a minute", that's his "oh shit" moment, where he realises he isn't going to Heaven (at least, not just yet.)
Him going "this is going to get complicated" could be another "oh shit" moment, though smaller as he realises the female narrator is stalling his exist from purgatory.
He tries to toy with the female narrator, trick her mind, throw her off-guard by turning the rubber band into a sphere. Sometimes, she falls for it, others she throws right into the male narrator's face, with him calling it out as "bullshit!".
4:13 The female narrator undoing his band work is her way of stalling and keeping him in purgatory, to which the male narrator objects.
4:58 The male narrator saying "is this it?" is him preparing for the end of his purgatory, until the female narrator tells him it isn't done just yet, which leads into the end segment.
Finally, the end of the video with the casket is symbolic of him finally escaping purgatory and making his way to Heaven.

Feel free to add onto this if you'd like. Hahaha.

text: @lol-de4lo  I think this was back in 7th or 6th grade, but the general idea is still there. Basically I had been messing around with the school's provided graphing calculators, not necessarily knowing what I was doing, but I did end up learning a few tricks that none of the other students knew at the time. They were life savers when the methods taught just took too long. After using these time saving tricks a while, I got fed up with how this one lesson was being taught. I demonstrated the tricks to the class (allowing them to bypass lots of work and get the answer). I was applauded for figuring it out by the teacher, but afterwards, those who used the methods were punished.
author: @gadget7888

text: @gadget7888  might be cause the school has to teach certain things
author: @lol-de4lo

text: @lol-de4lo  I know that's what it was, and i understand the action in hindsight. But it never ceased to bug me. Well, I defiantly kept to the methods, which carried me up through graduation.
